text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little town bordered by forests and rivers, there lived a little girl named Alice. She was known for her boundless imagination and for always hearing fantastical tales spun by her grandfather, who had a knack for folklore.

One crisp autumn morning, as leaves painted their yellows across the town, Alice stumbled upon an ancient book in her grandfather’s attic. Intrigued, she opened it. The pages were filled with drawings and faded text that spoke of an enchanted forest called Everdune. It was said that within Everdune lived a gentle fairy known as Glenda.

Alice longed to meet Glenda and had decided to set out on an adventure. Clutching the book closely, she donned her prettiest green dress and grabbed her tricorn hat. Her father gave her a map and a lantern before he went back to the farm to prep for the journey.
